description:
Create a GitLab CI pipeline with variables DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive and LC_ALL=C.UTF-8, defining two stages: test (default) and deploy. In the test stage add a fdroid-website job using the debian:bookworm image that runs the following commands in order: apt-get -q update; apt-get -qy install bundler git ruby --no-install-recommends; git -C .. clone --depth=1 https://gitlab.com/fdroid/fdroid-website.git; cd ../fdroid-website; sed -i -e 's,^languages:,ignored_languages:,' -e 's,^gitlab_ci_languages:,languages:,' _config.yml; perl -i -pe "BEGIN{undef $/;} s@
 *gem 'jekyll-fdroid'.*?
\s*:ref *=> *'[^']+'
@
@smg" Gemfile; sed -i "s@^end@    gem 'jekyll-fdroid', path\x3a '$CI_PROJECT_DIR'
end@" Gemfile; $CI_PROJECT_DIR/.ci-get-from-fdroid-website.rb | bash -e; cd $CI_PROJECT_DIR; rubocop lib spec; rspec; cd ../fdroid-website; sed -Ei -e "s,^(url\x3a).*,\1 'https://$CI_PROJECT_NAMESPACE.gitlab.io'," -e "s,^(baseurl\x3a).*,\1 '/$CI_PROJECT_NAME'," _config.yml; echo "Jekyll config used for CI:" && cat _config.yml; mkdir $CI_PROJECT_DIR/public; ln -s $CI_PROJECT_DIR/public; jekyll build -d public --trace --future; cp -v robots.txt.noindex public/robots.txt; ./tools/prepare-multi-lang.sh public --no-type-maps; apt-get install curl; mkdir -p $CI_PROJECT_DIR/public/css; curl https://fdroid.gitlab.io/fdroid-website/css/main.css > $CI_PROJECT_DIR/public/css/main.css; and publishes the public directory as an artifact. Also in the test stage add a ruby_latest job using the ruby image that runs: ruby -v; for f in `find * -name *.rb`; do printf "$f	"; ruby -c $f; done; apt-get update; apt-get install -y bundler; bundle install; bundle exec rubocop lib spec; bundle exec rspec;. In the deploy stage add a pages job that runs ls -lR public, always uploads the public directory as a GitLab Pages artifact with a one‑week expiration.
